title: UAE approves first nasal adrenaline spray for severe allergies in regional first - Arabian Business: Latest
summary: UAE approves EURneffy, the region’s first nasal adrenaline spray for severe allergies, boosting emergency care response and patient safety
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
The UAE has approved the region’s first nasal adrenaline spray for the emergency treatment of severe allergic reactions, marking a significant step forward in patient care and medical innovation.

The Emirates Drug Establishment (EDE) granted approval for EURneffy, an epinephrine-based treatment available in 1 mg and 2 mg strengths, positioning the UAE as the first country in the region to authorise this new therapy.

EURneffy is the first nasal adrenaline spray indicated for emergency treatment of severe allergic reactions in adults and children aged four years and above, with dosing determined based on body weight.

Developed by ALK Abelló, the treatment offers an alternative to traditional injectable epinephrine, enabling administration through the nasal route.

This innovation supports faster responses in acute allergic emergencies, where timely delivery of epinephrine is critical to saving lives.

The nasal spray is designed for ease of use and rapid administration, allowing immediate intervention even by non-medically trained individuals.

It addresses practical challenges associated with injectable treatments, including hesitation due to needle-related anxiety and difficulties in administering injections during critical situations.

By simplifying delivery, EURneffy aims to reduce response time and improve survival outcomes and overall patient safety.

The Emirates Drug Establishment said the approval reflects its efforts to strengthen healthcare system readiness and expand access to innovative treatment options.

Dr Fatima Al Kaabi, Director General of the Emirates Drug Establishment, said: “The decision to grant approval for this treatment was driven by the UAE’s strategic direction to accelerate the adoption of innovative therapies with a direct impact on patients’ lives. Providing advanced and easy-to-use treatment options in critical situations is a priority, given their decisive role in improving response rates and reducing risks associated with delayed intervention.”

Healthcare innovation

Al Kaabi added: “The UAE’s approval of the region’s first nasal adrenaline spray demonstrates the agility and efficiency of the country’s regulatory framework and its ability to attract and approve innovative therapeutic solutions. This enhances the quality of healthcare and strengthens readiness in managing critical cases.”

The product was introduced to the Emirates Drug Establishment during a recent visit to Denmark, with the authority working closely with the manufacturer to accelerate its availability to patients in the UAE.

The approval supports patient safety and improves quality of life by enabling rapid access to life-saving treatment.

Dr Al Kaabi said the authority will continue to facilitate access to the latest therapies through advanced regulatory processes that ensure speed and precision in evaluation and approval.

Ghassan Al Qatib, Regional General Manager at ALK Abelló, said: “Having EURneffy approved for use in the UAE is undoubtedly an important step towards improving access for patients and healthcare providers to effective and easy-to-use treatment options in emergency situations.”

Al Qatib added: “We remain committed to delivering innovative therapies that meet patient needs in collaboration with our partners. Our cooperation with the Emirates Drug Establishment reflects a shared commitment to advancing pharmaceutical care in the UAE and across the region.”

With the approval of EURneffy, the UAE reinforces its position as a leading hub for medical innovation, supported by a flexible regulatory framework that enables rapid access to advanced therapies while maintaining high standards of quality and safety.
